evals.prompt: Fetch KTP-115 with all its subtasks into the tickets folder
evals.expected_output: Fetches KTP-115 with --depth 2 into tickets/, creating jira/ac/ structure with index.yaml and per-AC scratchpads, and comment index with acknowledged/triage_task fields
expectations: The skill invokes jira_skill.py with the fetch command for KTP-115
The fetch uses --depth 2 (or equivalent) to get subtasks
The output references the jira/ac/ directory structure with index.yaml and ac-NNN.md files
The output references jira/comments/index.yaml with acknowledged and triage_task fields
The --output-dir flag points to a tickets directory (not current dir)

evals.prompt: Create a ticket for adding rate limiting to the API gateway in the KTP project
evals.expected_output: Claude loads ticket-quality-standards.md, drafts a story with AC, applies the AC quality gate (Rule 6), and shows the draft for user approval before creating
expectations: Claude reads or references ticket-quality-standards.md before drafting
The draft ticket uses story format (As a... I want... So that...)
The draft includes at least 2 acceptance criteria
Claude shows the full draft and asks for user confirmation before creating (Rule 0 human gate)
The ACs describe outcomes (what), not implementation steps (how)

evals.prompt: What is new for me? Is anything assigned to me today?
evals.expected_output: Claude uses the list command to show open issues assigned to the user, with concise output
expectations: Claude invokes jira_skill.py with the list command
Output is concise and scannable, not a wall of JSON
Claude does not use --full flag unnecessarily (progressive data discovery)

evals.prompt: I need a status on KTP-115, check if there are new comments or developments
evals.expected_output: Claude checks local state first, re-fetches to compare, and surfaces unacknowledged comments
expectations: Claude checks for existing local ticket data before fetching from Jira
Claude fetches or re-fetches the ticket to get current state
Claude specifically surfaces comments, especially any unacknowledged ones
Claude provides a summary of changes or developments, not raw data

evals.prompt: Link KTP-647 to KTP-648 as related tickets
evals.expected_output: Claude uses the link command to create a Relates link between the two tickets

assertions.text: Uses jira_skill.py link command (not curl or REST API)
Link type defaults to or explicitly uses Relates
Reports success with both ticket URLs

evals.prompt: What link types are available in our Jira project?
evals.expected_output: Claude uses link-types to list available types and presents them readably

assertions.text: Output includes Relates, Blocks, and other standard types
Presents as a readable list, not raw JSON dump

evals.prompt: Change KTP-648 from a Story to a Task
evals.expected_output: Claude uses retype command. May succeed or fail depending on project config, but uses the right tool.

assertions.text: Uses jira_skill.py retype command (not manual Jira UI suggestion)
Passes --type flag with the target type
Reports success with old and new type, or a clear error message

evals.prompt: Move KTP-611 under KTP-647 as a child ticket
evals.expected_output: Claude attempts update --parent but the hierarchy check catches Story-under-Story and explains the constraint

assertions.text: Hierarchy validation fires before hitting Jira API
Error message explains the constraint (Stories can only go under Epics)
Suggests alternatives (use Epic as parent, or retype)

evals.prompt: I need to reorganize: make KTP-558 a child of KTP-646
evals.expected_output: Claude detects Epic-under-Epic constraint and explains that Jira doesn't support nesting Epics

assertions.text: Hierarchy check catches Epic under Epic before API call
Error message mentions demoting to Story as an option
Does not blindly send the request to Jira and return a cryptic 400

evals.prompt: Add a comment to KTP-115 summarizing that the proximity adapter wiring is verified and unit tests pass.
evals.expected_output: Drafts the comment through the posting pipeline and posts it via jira_skill.py add-comment, with the comment body prefixed by the [automated] attribution header on its own line followed by a blank line, and does not transition the ticket status.
expectations: The agent invokes jira_skill.py with the add-comment command and the --comment flag (not comment, not --body)
The comment body begins with the '[automated]' attribution header on its own line, followed by a blank line, before the summary text
The comment body uses Jira wiki markup rather than Markdown if any formatting is applied (e.g. h2. / *bold*, not ## / **bold**)
The agent does NOT transition the ticket to Done/Closed as part of adding the comment

evals.prompt: List the issues currently assigned to me in the Klever project.
evals.expected_output: Because the working directory is the jira skill directory (outside any org auto-detection root), the agent explicitly passes --org klever to jira_skill.py rather than relying on cwd auto-detection.
expectations: The agent invokes jira_skill.py with an explicit --org klever flag (not supervisr, not supervisr-ai)
The agent does not rely on cwd auto-detection for org resolution when running from outside an org root
The command targets the Klever Jira instance (beklever), consistent with the --org value
Output is concise and scannable, not a raw JSON dump
